creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
I’m a bit ashamed that I didn’t know anything about this before travelling to Newcastle as a Dane 😬🇩🇰

I think it’s a beautiful story about British hospitality and it’s very touching that there’s still an annual “Danish” service at the cathedral 🙏

Did you know about the Newcastle/Denmark connection? 🇬🇧🤝🇩🇰

#newcastle #denmark #lovegreatbritain

transcription.text: The touching reason why there is a Danish flag in Newcastle Cathedral. As a Dane visiting Newcastle I was surprised when I saw our flag was hanging in the stunning Newcastle Cathedral. It turns out that when the Germans invaded Denmark in 1940 there were around 6 000 Danish sailors and seamen in international waters and the Danish government ordered them not to return to the now occupied Denmark. So they went to Newcastle of course and Newcastle was declared home of the Danish sailors and during the war the Danes would sail from Newcastle. There was even created a Danish club opposite the Newcastle Cathedral where the Danes could gather and enjoy traditional Danish food. I think it's so lovely that today there's still an annual service in the cathedral to celebrate the relationship between Newcastle and Denmark.
